title: Ptilagrostis concinna
intro: Ptilagrostis concinna, also known as Purple Needlegrass, is a perennial grass native to North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory ailments, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ptilagrostis concinna include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to relieve coughs, colds, and flu symptoms.
taxonomy: Ptilagrostis concinna belongs to the family Poaceae. It is a species of grass. Ptilagrostis concinna is a member of the subfamily Pooideae. Further classification includes the tribe Poeae and the subtribe Poinae. Ptilagrostis concinna is native to North America.
